revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 広島県三次市十日市南四丁目7-55 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[誉田別尊]] |社格 = [[旧村社]] |創建 = 保元年間（12世紀中半）『広島県神社誌』『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 |本殿 = 入母屋造銅板葺 |別名 = 若宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = }} '''若宮八幡神社'''（わかみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[三次市]]十日市に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧称'''鏡山'''（かがみやま）'''八幡神社'''、「'''若宮さん'''」と通称される[[旧村社]]。 [[三次盆地]]の南縁から北に張り出した丘陵の突端部に鎮座する。境内は[[桜]]の名所として知られ『広島県の地名』、若宮公園ここも[[尾関山公園]]に並ぶ桜の名所である。が隣接する。 == 祭神 == 誉田別尊（[[応神天皇]]、[[八幡神]]）を主祭神に、気長足姫尊（[[神功皇后]]）、[[仲姫命]]、[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰大臣]]、足仲彦尊（[[仲哀天皇]]）、[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫尊]]、[[少彦名神]]、[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[中臣常磐|中臣常磐大連公]]（なかとみのときわのおおむらじこう）を[[相殿|相殿神]]として祀り、[[伊弉諾尊]]、[[伊弉冉尊]]、[[栲幡千千姫命]]、[[木花咲耶姫命]]、[[大山祇命]]、[[軻遇突智命]]を[[合祀]]する。 == 由緒 == *[[保元|保元年中]]（1156年-59年）に畠敷村の[[比叡尾山城|比叡尾山（ひえびやま）城]][[城主]]である三吉兼連（みよしかねつら）が男山八幡宮を勧請した。『広島県神社誌』『双三郡史国群誌』『[[芸藩通志]]』に因れば[[鎌倉時代]]の初、三吉村（後の原村、現三次市十日町や三次町一帯）の[[地頭]]である三吉兼範が[[山城国]][[石清水八幡宮|男山八幡宮]]を原村の救山（すくいやま）に[[勧請]]したのを創祀と伝える。 *[[天文 (元号)|天文]]4年（1524年）[[室町時代]]に兵火により罹災した為に三吉致高により鏡山八幡神社として新しく社殿が造立される。『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 *[[弘治 (日本)|弘治]]3年（1557年）9月3日に三吉氏14代[[三吉隆亮|隆亮]]が上里村（あがりむら）寺戸（現三次町寺戸願万地）へ遷し、その折の[[棟札]]が遺されていた。『国郡志御用ニ付下調書出帳』上里村分、同帳は[[文政]]2年（1819）から翌3年にかけて『芸藩通志』（文政8年完成）編纂の基礎資料として広島藩管下の各村から藩へ提出された書出 *[[天正]]10年（1582年）に隆亮が寄進した鉄燈籠も遺されるが、この時は上里村八幡神社と呼ばれ、当時は神田として毎年200[[石 (単位)|石]]を[[社領]]としていた。『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』なお、救山から上里村への遷座に際しては御[[神体]]が途中寺戸の農家に1年近く逗留したとの事で、その縁からその農家の当主が下述する神幸祭において代々重役を勤める事となったという前掲『国郡志御用ニ付下調書出帳』。 *[[享保]]2年（1717年）に[[浅野長澄]]により社殿が修復された。『広島県神社誌』『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』近世には上里村、三次町、原村と東河内村（現東河内町）半分の[[産土神]]とされた。 *[[明治]]になって村社に列し、[[大正]]4年（1915年）、双三郡原町内の愛宕神社（軻遇突智命）、大山神社（大山祇命）、太歳神社（木花咲耶姫命）、若宮神社（伊弉諾尊・伊弉冉尊）、救神社を合祀し、翌5年10月に現社地へ遷座した。 *[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に拝殿が新築された。なお、昭和初期までは鏡山八幡神社と呼ばれていた。 現代では特に正月から1月末や秋季大祭式例祭が行われる。 == 祭祀 == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; line-height:1.4em; font-size:0.7em; margin:0px 0px 3px 7px;" |+祭事暦 | rowspan=3 | 1月 || 1日 || 歳旦祭 |- | 2日～5日頃 || 正月祭 |- | 14日 || [[とんど|とんど祭]] |- | 2月 || 3日 || [[節分祭]] |- | rowspan=2 | 4月 || 3日 || [[上巳|上巳の節句]]の祭（人形慰靈祭） |- | 29日 || 天神・大仙神社祭 |- | 5月 || 上旬 || 春季大祭 |- | 7月 || 中旬 || 夏季大祭 |- | rowspan=2 | 8月 || 7日頃 || [[七夕]]祭 |- | 15日 || 忠魂社御霊祭 |- | 10月 || 第3日曜日 || 秋季大祭（例祭） |- | rowspan=2 | 11月 || 月中 || [[七五三|七五三祭]] |- | 下旬 || 摂末社祭 |- | 12月 || 31日 || [[大祓|大祓式]]除夜祭 |- | colspan=2 | 毎月1日（1月は5～6日頃） || [[月次祭]] |} 10月の例祭に[[神輿]]による[[神幸]]が行われる。神輿4基による渡御で、当初は9月9日に発輿して隔年で三次町五日市の[[太歳神社]]と下述岩神弁才天社を[[お旅所]]として駐輿し、15日に還御していたが、[[宝暦]]7年（1757年）からは旧鎮座地である救山への渡御が行われるようになり、[[寛政]]7年（1795年）からは9月14日に太歳神社で1泊、翌15日に救山へ渡御した後に還御する形となった。近世には神輿に神霊を遷す役である大長役は上述した寺戸の農家の当主が勤める習いで、一説に弘治 3年（1557年）の農家に逗留した故事同様、往時道中で神輿が4基共に動かなくなった事もあったという。 {{-}} == 社殿 == 本殿は方3間の[[入母屋造]]平入、正面に[[千鳥破風]]を飾り軒唐破風の[[向拝]]を付ける。 == 摂末社 == *淡島神社 :[[少彦名神]]を祀る。[[三次藩]]初代藩主[[浅野長治]]の女[[瑤泉院|阿久里姫]]の心願により3代藩主[[浅野長澄|長澄]]の代に[[紀伊国]][[加多浦]]の[[淡嶋神社|加太淡島神社]]の分霊を勧請したもの。『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 *荒神社 :[[素盞嗚尊]]他を祀る。平成廿二年七月に新たに社殿を新築している。 *稲荷神社 :[[保食神]]（稲荷神）を祀る。淡島神社の右側に座している。 *天神社・大仙神社 :天神社は[[菅原道真|菅原道真公]]を、大仙社は大山祇命を祀る。 *粟屋神社 :[[粟屋隆信|粟屋（三吉）刑部丞隆信（あわやぎょうぶのしょうたかのぶ）公]]と、相殿に三吉式部少輔（しきぶのしょう）藤原隆亮（ふじわらたかすけ）公（上記三吉隆亮の神霊）を祀る。旧称粟屋大明神。粟屋隆信の菩提寺である粟屋町上村の法雲山龍興寺の跡に彼の墓がある『粟屋村国群誌』。三次市畠敷町の岩屋寺展望台には彼の慰霊碑がある。 *忠魂社 :560柱の殉死者を祀り毎年8月15日に忠魂社御霊祭が執り行われる。 === 嚴島神社 === {{神社 |名称 = 嚴島神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Itsukushima_shrine_miyoshi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 広島県三次市十日市中四丁目7-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|29.8|N|132|51|06.0|E|scale:25000|name=嚴島神社 (三次市十日市中)}} |祭神 = [[市杵島姫命]] |社格 = |創建 = [[寛永|寛永年間]]（17世紀前葉） |本殿 = 入母屋造銅板葺 |別名 = 弁天さん |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月下旬 |神事 = 輪くぐり祭（6月30日） }} '''嚴島神社'''（いつくしまじんじゃ）は十日市中に鎮座する境外所管社。[[寛永]]年中（1624-44年）に備後出羽庭村'''万福寺'''が弁財天[[供僧寺]]として現在の巴橋の袂に移転してきたものである。[[市杵島姫命]]を祭神とするが、元は[[弁才天]]を祀る寺で'''岩神弁才天'''と呼ばれていた。安芸、石見や出雲の商人により神殿を造営され、浅野長治により神殿の拡張が行われている。 [[安芸国]]を始め[[出雲国]]からも参拝者が絶えず殷賑を極め、5月初子の日を市入祭、7月初子の日を水運祭として商売繁盛等を祈願する大祭が行われていた。明治初期の[[神仏分離]]により神社となったが、その後も「'''弁天さん'''」と通称されて親しまれている。現在の神殿は[[文化 (元号)|文化]]8年（1811年）に再建され、昭和47年の洪水により同50年に現在地に遷座している。『広島県神社誌』『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 境内に恵比須神社が鎮座し、11月19、20両日には恵比須神社祭（えびす講）が斎行される。 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『広島県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系第35巻）平凡社、1982年 == 交通 == *JR[[芸備線]][[三次駅]]より徒歩20分 === 周辺情報 === *十日市 **[[若宮古墳]]・花園遺跡 **[[三次人形|三次人形窯元]]（江戸時代より続く県内唯一の人形窯元）（[http://www.miyoshi-ningyou.com// 三次人形窯元]） **金峯山修験本宗別格本山成道山[[日光寺 (三次市十日市)| 日光寺]]（中国四十九薬師霊場　第14番札所） *三次町 **[[鳳源寺]] **[[吉祥院（三次市三次町）|吉祥院]]（備後西国三十三観音霊場　第16番札所・中国楽寿三十三観音霊場　第13番札所） **[[太歳神社]] *畠敷周辺 **[[熊野神社 (三次市畠敷町)|熊野神社]] **[[岩屋寺（三次市畠敷町）|岩屋寺]]（備後西国三十三観音霊場　第17番札所） **[[広島県立みよし公園]]　 *東酒屋町 **[[三次ワイナリー]]（[http://www.miyoshi-wine.co.jp/ 広島三次ワイナリー]） **[[奥田元宋・小由女美術館]]（[http://genso-sayume.jp/index.html/ 奥田元宋・小由女美術館]） **[[みよし運動公園]] ==外部リンク== * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html 西国の山城　比叡尾山城（広島県三次市畠敷町）・その２ ] {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:わかみやはちまんしんしや みよししとおかいち}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県の旧村社]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:三次市の建築物]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:桜に関する場所]]

{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 広島県三次市十日市南四丁目7-55 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = [[誉田別尊]] |社格 = [[旧村社]] |創建 = 保元年間（12世紀中半）『広島県神社誌』『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 |本殿 = 入母屋造銅板葺 |別名 = 若宮さん |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第3日曜日 |神事 = }} '''若宮八幡神社'''（わかみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[三次市]]十日市に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧称'''鏡山'''（かがみやま）'''八幡神社'''、「'''若宮さん'''」と通称される[[旧村社]]。 [[三次盆地]]の南縁から北に張り出した丘陵の突端部に鎮座する。境内は[[桜]]の名所として知られ『広島県の地名』、若宮公園ここも[[尾関山公園]]に並ぶ桜の名所である。が隣接する。 == 祭神 == 誉田別尊（[[応神天皇]]、[[八幡神]]）を主祭神に、気長足姫尊（[[神功皇后]]）、[[仲姫命]]、[[武内宿禰|武内宿禰大臣]]、足仲彦尊（[[仲哀天皇]]）、[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫尊]]、[[少彦名神]]、[[素盞嗚尊]]、[[中臣常磐|中臣常磐大連公]]（なかとみのときわのおおむらじこう）を[[相殿|相殿神]]として祀り、[[伊弉諾尊]]、[[伊弉冉尊]]、[[栲幡千千姫命]]、[[木花咲耶姫命]]、[[大山祇命]]、[[軻遇突智命]]を[[合祀]]する。 == 由緒 == *[[保元|保元年中]]（1156年-59年）に畠敷村の[[比叡尾山城|比叡尾山（ひえびやま）城]][[城主]]である三吉兼連（みよしかねつら）が男山八幡宮を勧請した。『広島県神社誌』『双三郡史国群誌』『[[芸藩通志]]』に因れば[[鎌倉時代]]の初、三吉村（後の原村、現三次市十日町や三次町一帯）の[[地頭]]である三吉兼範が[[山城国]][[石清水八幡宮|男山八幡宮]]を原村の救山（すくいやま）に[[勧請]]したのを創祀と伝える。 *[[天文 (元号)|天文]]4年（1524年）[[室町時代]]に兵火により罹災した為に三吉致高により鏡山八幡神社として新しく社殿が造立される。『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 *[[弘治 (日本)|弘治]]3年（1557年）9月3日に三吉氏14代[[三吉隆亮|隆亮]]が上里村（あがりむら）寺戸（現三次町寺戸願万地）へ遷し、その折の[[棟札]]が遺されていた。『国郡志御用ニ付下調書出帳』上里村分、同帳は[[文政]]2年（1819）から翌3年にかけて『芸藩通志』（文政8年完成）編纂の基礎資料として広島藩管下の各村から藩へ提出された書出 *[[天正]]10年（1582年）に隆亮が寄進した鉄燈籠も遺されるが、この時は上里村八幡神社と呼ばれ、当時は神田として毎年200[[石 (単位)|石]]を[[社領]]としていた。『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』なお、救山から上里村への遷座に際しては御[[神体]]が途中寺戸の農家に1年近く逗留したとの事で、その縁からその農家の当主が下述する神幸祭において代々重役を勤める事となったという前掲『国郡志御用ニ付下調書出帳』。 *[[享保]]2年（1717年）に[[浅野長澄]]により社殿が修復された。『広島県神社誌』『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』近世には上里村、三次町、原村と東河内村（現東河内町）半分の[[産土神]]とされた。 *[[明治]]になって村社に列し、[[大正]]4年（1915年）、双三郡原町内の愛宕神社（軻遇突智命）、大山神社（大山祇命）、太歳神社（木花咲耶姫命）、若宮神社（伊弉諾尊・伊弉冉尊）、救神社を合祀し、翌5年10月に現社地へ遷座した。 *[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に拝殿が新築された。なお、昭和初期までは鏡山八幡神社と呼ばれていた。 現代では特に正月から1月末や秋季大祭式例祭が行われる。 == 祭祀 == {|class="wikitable" style="float:right; line-height:1.4em; font-size:0.7em; margin:0px 0px 3px 7px;" |+祭事暦 | rowspan=3 | 1月 || 1日 || 歳旦祭 |- | 2日～5日頃 || 正月祭 |- | 14日 || [[とんど|とんど祭]] |- | 2月 || 3日 || [[節分祭]] |- | rowspan=2 | 4月 || 3日 || [[上巳|上巳の節句]]の祭（人形慰靈祭） |- | 29日 || 天神・大仙神社祭 |- | 5月 || 上旬 || 春季大祭 |- | 7月 || 中旬 || 夏季大祭 |- | rowspan=2 | 8月 || 7日頃 || [[七夕]]祭 |- | 15日 || 忠魂社御霊祭 |- | 10月 || 第3日曜日 || 秋季大祭（例祭） |- | rowspan=2 | 11月 || 月中 || [[七五三|七五三祭]] |- | 下旬 || 摂末社祭 |- | 12月 || 31日 || [[大祓|大祓式]]除夜祭 |- | colspan=2 | 毎月1日（1月は5～6日頃） || [[月次祭]] |} 10月の例祭に[[神輿]]による[[神幸]]が行われる。神輿4基による渡御で、当初は9月9日に発輿して隔年で三次町五日市の[[太歳神社]]と下述岩神弁才天社を[[お旅所]]として駐輿し、15日に還御していたが、[[宝暦]]7年（1757年）からは旧鎮座地である救山への渡御が行われるようになり、[[寛政]]7年（1795年）からは9月14日に太歳神社で1泊、翌15日に救山へ渡御した後に還御する形となった。近世には神輿に神霊を遷す役である大長役は上述した寺戸の農家の当主が勤める習いで、一説に弘治 3年（1557年）の農家に逗留した故事同様、往時道中で神輿が4基共に動かなくなった事もあったという。 {{-}} == 社殿 == 本殿は方3間の[[入母屋造]]平入、正面に[[千鳥破風]]を飾り軒唐破風の[[向拝]]を付ける。 == 摂末社 == *淡島神社 :[[少彦名神]]を祀る。[[三次藩]]初代藩主[[浅野長治]]の女[[瑤泉院|阿久里姫]]の心願により3代藩主[[浅野長澄|長澄]]の代に[[紀伊国]][[加多浦]]の[[淡嶋神社|加太淡島神社]]の分霊を勧請したもの。『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 *荒神社 :[[素盞嗚尊]]他を祀る。平成廿二年七月に新たに社殿を新築している。 *稲荷神社 :[[保食神]]（稲荷神）を祀る。淡島神社の右側に座している。 *天神社・大仙神社 :天神社は[[菅原道真|菅原道真公]]を、大仙社は大山祇命を祀る。 *粟屋神社 :[[粟屋隆信|粟屋（三吉）刑部丞隆信（あわやぎょうぶのしょうたかのぶ）公]]と、相殿に三吉式部少輔（しきぶのしょう）藤原隆亮（ふじわらたかすけ）公（上記三吉隆亮の神霊）を祀る。旧称粟屋大明神。粟屋隆信の菩提寺である粟屋町上村の法雲山龍興寺の跡に彼の墓がある『粟屋村国群誌』。三次市畠敷町の岩屋寺展望台には彼の慰霊碑がある。 *忠魂社 :560柱の殉死者を祀り毎年8月15日に忠魂社御霊祭が執り行われる。 === 嚴島神社 === {{神社 |名称 = 嚴島神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Itsukushima_shrine_miyoshi.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = 広島県三次市十日市中四丁目7-11 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|48|29.8|N|132|51|06.0|E|scale:25000|name=嚴島神社 (三次市十日市中)}} |祭神 = [[市杵島姫命]] |社格 = |創建 = [[寛永|寛永年間]]（17世紀前葉） |本殿 = 入母屋造銅板葺 |別名 = 弁天さん |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月下旬 |神事 = 輪くぐり祭（6月30日） }} '''嚴島神社'''（いつくしまじんじゃ）は十日市中に鎮座する境外所管社。[[寛永]]年中（1624-44年）に備後出羽庭村'''万福寺'''が弁財天[[供僧寺]]として現在の巴橋の袂に移転してきたものである。[[市杵島姫命]]を祭神とするが、元は[[弁才天]]を祀る寺で'''岩神弁才天'''と呼ばれていた。安芸、石見や出雲の商人により神殿を造営され、浅野長治により神殿の拡張が行われている。 [[安芸国]]を始め[[出雲国]]からも参拝者が絶えず殷賑を極め、5月初子の日を市入祭、7月初子の日を水運祭として商売繁盛等を祈願する大祭が行われていた。明治初期の[[神仏分離]]により神社となったが、その後も「'''弁天さん'''」と通称されて親しまれている。現在の神殿は[[文化 (元号)|文化]]8年（1811年）に再建され、昭和47年の洪水により同50年に現在地に遷座している。『広島県神社誌』『芸藩通誌』『双三郡史国群誌』 境内に恵比須神社が鎮座し、11月19、20両日には恵比須神社祭（えびす講）が斎行される。 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『広島県の地名』（日本歴史地名大系第35巻）平凡社、1982年 == 交通 == *JR[[芸備線]][[三次駅]]より徒歩20分 === 周辺情報 === *十日市 **[[若宮古墳 (三次市)|若宮古墳]]・花園遺跡 **[[三次人形|三次人形窯元]]（江戸時代より続く県内唯一の人形窯元）（[http://www.miyoshi-ningyou.com// 三次人形窯元]） **金峯山修験本宗別格本山成道山[[日光寺 (三次市十日市)| 日光寺]]（中国四十九薬師霊場　第14番札所） *三次町 **[[鳳源寺]] **[[吉祥院（三次市三次町）|吉祥院]]（備後西国三十三観音霊場　第16番札所・中国楽寿三十三観音霊場　第13番札所） **[[太歳神社]] *畠敷周辺 **[[熊野神社 (三次市畠敷町)|熊野神社]] **[[岩屋寺（三次市畠敷町）|岩屋寺]]（備後西国三十三観音霊場　第17番札所） **[[広島県立みよし公園]]　 *東酒屋町 **[[三次ワイナリー]]（[http://www.miyoshi-wine.co.jp/ 広島三次ワイナリー]） **[[奥田元宋・小由女美術館]]（[http://genso-sayume.jp/index.html/ 奥田元宋・小由女美術館]） **[[みよし運動公園]] ==外部リンク== * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html 西国の山城　比叡尾山城（広島県三次市畠敷町）・その２ ] {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:わかみやはちまんしんしや みよししとおかいち}} [[Category:広島県の神社]] [[Category:広島県の旧村社]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] revision.text:
[[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |Image = [[File:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |Location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: * Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) * Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) * Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) * Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) * Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) * Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) * Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) * Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon Sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

Haiden |Location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: * Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) * Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) * Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) * Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) * Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) * Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) * Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) * Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon Sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

[[category:new pages]][[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |Image = [[File:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |Location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: * Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) * Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) * Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) * Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) * Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) * Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) * Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) * Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

Haiden |Location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

Haiden |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

Haiden |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]]

[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] {{Shrine |Name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine |image = [[File:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Hondawake no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT::Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place Names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{DEFAULTSORT::Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

Haiden |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]]{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]]{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==

[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]]{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:dd]][[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Miyoshi|ja|三次市}}, Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the {{ill|Hōgen Era|ja|保元}} (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}}."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文}} 4 (1524), during the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}, the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正}} 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR {{ill|Geibi Line|ja|芸備線}} {{ill|Miyoshi Station|ja|三次駅}} == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Miyoshi}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)-qq]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]]{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:last refresh attempt]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] ==References==
