Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]] == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] ==References== {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)|version=101725335|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] [[en:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[Category:三次市の建築物]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:広島県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]]

According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]] == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] ==References== {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)|version=101725335|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Cherry blossom spots]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[Category:Buildings in Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:広島県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]]

According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]] == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] ==References== {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)|version=101725335|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[Category:Buildings in Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:三次市の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616259]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34|48|02.0|N|132|51|28.2|E|display=inline,title|scale:25000}} |Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry""Gehan Tsuushi""Futami County History and Village Chronicles" |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October }} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ}} viewing site"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names.", adjacent to Wakamiya ParkThis site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公) Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命) == History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]]."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. * In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare."Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." * In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi."Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era) * By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern. * In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine."Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry.""Gehan Tsuushi.""Futami County History and Village Chronicles." == Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November) == Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing. == Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582) == Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]] == External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)] ==References== {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)|version=101725335|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(三次市十日市)]] [[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman Shrine (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]] [[Category:Buildings in Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Miyoshi]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hanami spots of Japan]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社 (三次市十日市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616259]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Wakamiya-yahatajinja.jpg |location = 7-55 Tokaiichi Minami 4-chome, Miyoshi, Hiroshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|34.800556|N|132.857833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Former Village Shrine]] |Foundation = Hōgen Era (mid-12th century)<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry"</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi"</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles"</ref> |MainHall = Irimoya-zukuri with copper roofing |Alias = Wakamiya-san |Festival = 3rd Sunday of October |prefecture= {{ill|Miyoshi|qid=Q840591}} |deity= {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]
==References==

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=|1=Kōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>

Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]

== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]
==References==

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)

Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|qq=|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|qq=|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|qq=|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|qq=|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)

* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>

== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.

Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)

* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>

Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
== Deities == The shrine enshrines: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊) - [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], revered as Hachiman. * Other enshrined deities include: ** Jingu Kōgō (神功皇后) ** Nakatsuhime no Mikoto (仲姫命) ** Takeuchi no Sukune (武内宿禰) ** Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto (足仲彦尊/仲哀天皇) ** Tamayorihime no Mikoto (玉依姫尊) ** Sukunahikona no Mikoto (少彦名神) ** Susanoo no Mikoto (素盞嗚尊) ** Nakatomi Tokiwano Omuraji no Kō (中臣常磐大連公)
Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)

* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>

Additionally, several deities are enshrined together: * Izanagi no Mikoto (伊弉諾尊) * Izanami no Mikoto (伊弉冉尊) * Takuhatachijihime no Mikoto (栲幡千千姫命) * Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto (木花咲耶姫命) * Ōyama-tsumi no Mikoto (大山祇命) * Kagutsuchi no Mikoto (軻遇突智命)
== History == * During the [[:en:Hōgen (era)|Hōgen Era]] (1156–1159), Kanetsura Miyoshi, lord of {{ill|Hiebyō Castle|ja|比叡尾山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiebyō Castle|1=Hiebyō Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55524298}} in Hatajikimura, enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]].<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref> According to {{ill|Gehan Tsuushi|ja|芸藩通志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gehan Tsuushi|1=Geihan Tsuushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11615959}}, the shrine's origins trace back to Kanefusa Miyoshi inviting the deity to Sukuiyama during the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]].
* In {{ill|Tenbun|ja|天文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenbun|1=Tenbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21096812}} 4 (1524), during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the shrine was reconstructed by Michitaka Miyoshi after being damaged in warfare.<ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
* In {{ill|Kōji|ja|弘治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōji|1=kouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487351}} 3 (1557), the 14th head of the Miyoshi clan, Takanobu Miyoshi, relocated the shrine to Terado Village in Miyoshi.<ref>"Province and County Records for Official Use" (Bunsei Era)</ref>
* By [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 10 (1582), the shrine received significant contributions, including an iron lantern.<ref name="#1"/>
* In [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 2 (1717), Lord Nagachika Asano of Hiroshima restored the shrine.<ref name="hirosima">"Hiroshima Prefecture Shrine Registry."</ref><ref name="geihan">"Gehan Tsuushi."</ref><ref name="futami">"Futami County History and Village Chronicles."</ref>
== Rituals == The shrine hosts numerous annual festivals, with the main event being the Autumn Grand Festival in October. Key ceremonies include: * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|ja|とんど祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagichō Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長}} (January 14) * Spring Grand Festival (April) * {{ill|Chinkon-sai|ja|忠魂社御霊祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinkon-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (August 15) * {{ill|Shichi-Go-San|ja|七五三祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichi-Go-San|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November)
== Shrine Buildings == The main sanctuary is a 3-bay irimoya-zukuri style with copper roofing.
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Iron Lantern|ja|鉄燈籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iron Lantern|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Tenshō 10 (1582)
== Access == * 20-minute walk from JR [[:en:Geibi Line|Geibi Line]] [[:en:Miyoshi Station (Hiroshima)|Miyoshi Station]]
== External Links == * [https://saigokunoyamajiro.blogspot.com/2012/11/blog-post_6.html Saigoku Mountain Castles - Hiebyō Castle (Miyoshi, Hiroshima)]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi]], Hiroshima Prefecture. Formerly known as '''Kagami-yama Hachiman Shrine''', it is commonly referred to as '''Wakamiya-san'''. It is classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
Situated at the northern edge of the Miyoshi Basin, the shrine sits atop a protruding hill. Its grounds are known as a popular {{ill|cherry blossom|ja|桜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cherry blossom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サクラ|1=cherry blossom|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q871991}} viewing site<ref name="#1">"Hiroshima Prefecture Place names."</ref>, adjacent to Wakamiya Park<ref>This site, like Ozaki-yama Park, is a renowned sakura viewing location.</ref>.
